ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
text: Jamɛ a be ɔ mi ɔ, Yesu he nɔ nɛ e de ke: “I ngɛ o yi jee, Tsaatsɛ, hiɔwe kɛ zugba nyɔmtsɛ, kaa o ngɔ ni nɛ ɔmɛ ngɔ laa ga leli kɛ ni leli, nɛ o je tsɔɔ bimwɔwi;
translated: One day, as Jesus was praying, one of his disciples came to him and said, "Lord, teach us to pray, just as John taught his disciples."

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: i hɛɛ ye he gu nɛ nyɛ wo mi tade; i nu hiɔ, nɛ nyɛ ba slaa mi; a wo mi tsu mi, nɛ nyɛ ba ye ngɔ.’
translated: You said you would go and I should wait for you; I waited, but you didn't come to meet me; you left me standing, and you didn't even look back.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Nɛ kpotoohi a kuu ngua ko ngɛ ni yee ngɛ lokoo.
translated: The elder who does not know death does not know life either.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: Nɛ kɛ ma mɔtu ngmlɛ nɛɛ mi ɔ, e je kpo ekohu nɛ e ya na ni kpahi komɛ nɛ a daa si yaka ngɛ jua a nɔ;
translated: And when I was a child my mother said, you will be a great person and you will have a lot of wisdom and you will be a leader and people will listen to you.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Tse nyɛ nu hulɔ kaa a de blematsɛmɛ ɔmɛ ke: ‘Ko ka kita gu, se mohu ye o kita amɛ a nɔ ha Nyɔmtsɛ ɔ.’
translated: “The chief said to the hunter, ‘Go and kill a lion, so that you will be called a hunter in the town of Nyɔmtsɛ.’”

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: Nɛ Yosef ngɔ nɔ gbogboe ɔ, nɛ e ngɔ kploba falefale ngɔ blaa lɛ,
translated: And Joseph's master took him, and put him into the prison, a place where the king's prisoners were bound.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Benɛ je bɔni jɔmi ɔ, e kaseli ɔmɛ ba e ngɔ ba de lɛ ke: “Nga nɔ ji hiɛ ɔ, nɛ je hu ngɛ jɔe;
translated: The palm tree said to the river, "When I am thirsty, I drink from you;

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Nɛ Yesu de mɛ ke: “Anɛ nɔ ko suɔ kane kɛ maa sowe loo sa tso sisi lo? Anɛ pi kane tso nɔ nɛ a kɛ maa lo?
translated: And Jesus said to them: "Who among you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone? Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Tse nyɛ nu nɛ a de ke: ‘Suɔ o huɛ nɛ o nyɛ o henyɛlɔ.’
translated: The chief says that: 'A stranger is a slave, and a slave is a servant.'

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Nɛ i kɛ lɛ ba o kaseli ɔmɛ a ngɔ, se a nyɛ we lɛ nɛ a tsa.”
translated: If you don't know how to talk, you can't claim to be wise, because wisdom comes from knowing how to speak.

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Nɛ Yesu fa mɛ ke a ha ni ɔmɛ tsuo nɛ a hi si kuuhi kuuhi ngɛ nga mumu ɔ nɔ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not without honor except in his own country and in his own house."

ref: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
text: Nɛ nɔ tsuaa nɔ nɛ maa nɔ tɛ nɛ ɔ nɔ ɔ, e mi ma tsɔtslɔɔ; se nɔ nɔ nɛ tɛ ɔ maa nɔ ɔ, e maa gblɛ lɛ mɔnyɔmɔnyɔ.]”
translated: If a child cries and the mother doesn't hear, the child will cry till it is tired; if the child doesn't cry and the mother doesn't hear, the mother will sleep soundly.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Nɛ nya tsɔlɔ ɔ ba e ngɔ ba de lɛ ke: “Ke Mawu bi ji mo ɔ, de konɛ tɛ nɛ ɔmɛ nɛ plɛ abolo.”
translated: The mother of the child said to her child: "If God does not give to you, nobody will give to you, so you have to pray to Him."

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: nɛ e fa kue ha lɛ wawɛɛ ke: “Ye biyo wayoo miɔ nɛ e ma gbo, he je ɔ ba nɛ o kɛ o ninehi ba pue e nɔ konɛ e na he wami nɛ e yi nɛ na wa.”
translated: The elders said to the chief: "If you want us to install you as chief, you must give us one of your daughters to be our wife and we will install you as chief."

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Se e he nɔ nɛ e de mɛ ke: “Gbɔkuɛ mi ɔ nyɛ deɔ ke: ‘Je mi ma tsɔ, ejakaa hiɔwe tsu.’
translated: He said to his mother: "Gbɔkuɛ told me to say: 'Let me go, I will go and play outside.'"

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Ekomɛ ya pue tɛ sahi a nɔ he nɛ zu fuu be. A puɛ oya, ejakaa zu ɔ mi kuɔ we.
translated: The chief of the town is not good and that is why the town is not developing. If he were good, the town would have developed long ago.

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Niheyo ɔ de lɛ ke: “Enɛ ɔmɛ tsuo ɔ, i ye nɔ kɛ je ye jokuɛwi a si; mɛni pa piɛ mi?”
translated: The chief said: "This is my river, I will not allow you to fish here; what is your authority?"

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: se mohu nyɛɛ ya Israel we tohi nɛ laa amɛ a ngɔ?
translated: What is the relationship between you and the people of Israel that they hate us so much?

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: “A gbaa ninɛmɛ nɛ a tsui mi tsɔ ɔ; se a maa na Mawu.
translated: If you do good and you are humble; that is how you will see God.

ref: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
text: Petro nyɛɛ e se ngɛ lokoo kɛ ba osɔfo nɔkɔtɔma a mɔ ɔ mi, nɛ e ya sɛ lejɛ ɔ nɛ e kɛ sɔmɔli ɔmɛ ya hii si, konɛ e hyɛ bɔ nɛ nyagbe ɔ maa ba ha.
translated: Peter is not a good person because he doesn't respect his pastor, and he doesn't like work, he only likes to play, so he will be poor and miserable all his life.
